title: Sida digitata
taxonomy: Sida digitata is classified within the family Malvaceae and is a member of the genus Sida. This classification places it within the order Malvales. Further classification at the genus level shows that it belongs to the tribe Malveae. As a member of the family Malvaceae, Sida digitata shares characteristics with other plants such as hibiscus and okra. Its classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological role.
uses: Sida digitata is known to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. The plant's leaves, roots, and stems are used to treat different health conditions. In some cultures, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Additionally, Sida digitata has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal benefits.
